details.text: Derivative: سَائِلٌ means a questioner but it implies someone that is cynical and is asking the question in mockery.
Derivative: حَمِيمٌ refers to a close and intimate friend.
Derivative: يَفْتَدِي from اِفْتَدَى means to ransom, to sacrifice or offer up something to attain something else.
Derivative: فَصِيلَتِ – فَصِيلَة refers to close family and relatives. It could also refer to clan and tribe.
Derivative: لَظَىٰ refers to fire that has no sparks coming out from it, a fire that has been burning for a while and is already raging and blazing.
Derivative: الشَّوَى refers to the body parts and sides, the limbs of the body like the arms and legs. It also can refer to the skin on the head or the body.
Derivative: نَزَّاعَةً is in the emphasised form (المُبَالَغَة – Al-Mubaalaghah) فَعَّال (Fa’aal). نَزَعَ means to strip and pull away and نَزَّاعَةً means to be continuously stripping away.
Derivative: أَوْعَىٰ literally means to place inside a container (وِعَاء - Wi’aa’) which is used to refer to storing and hoarding i.e., wealth.

Derivative: هَلُوعًا – Someone who has little patience, lacks balance and has no control over his temptations leading him to be greedy and miserly etc. The next to verses explain the meaning of هَلُوعًا.
Derivative: دَافِعٌ is someone who wards off and repels something.
Derivative: جَزُوعًا means to be impatient, fretful and in a state of being unhappy about things when they don’t go his way. It is accompanied by ingratitude and complaining a lot.

Derivative: مَنُوعًا means to be extremely preventing and resistant to others getting something. Here in respect to good (i.e., wealth) coming to him he does not want others to share in it and wants to keep it to himself i.e., to be greedy and miserly.
Derivative: مُّشْفِقُونَ is from أَشْفَقَ – إِشْفَاق which means to be fearful but also fearful for others in terms of being concerned and caring about them and having sympathy for them.

Derivative: الْمَعَارِجِ means great heights. عَرَجَ means to ascend up something like stairs. مَعَارِج refers to the great heights that are reached by going higher and higher or the ways which are ascended to reach the high heights.
Derivative: مَلُومِينَ – مَلُوم is the اِسْمُ المَفْعُول (Ismu-l-Maf’ul – Object of the verb) of the verb لَامَ – يَلُومُ which means to blame. مَلُوم means to be blamed or having blame attached.

Derivative: رَاعُونَ comes from رَعَى – يَرْعَى – رِعَايَةً which means to shepherd or tend to a flock of cattle. رَاعُونَ is the plural of رَاعٍ which a shepherd, protector or guardian. To be a guard over oaths and promises means to observe, fulfil and take care of them.
Derivative: Literally means to be standing however here it means to be upright, standing by the truth and giving testimony honestly and accurately without bias or concealing anything.

Derivative: مُهْطِعِينَ is from the verb أَهْطَعَ which means to run behind a leader obediently. مُهْطِعِينَ has been said to mean “rushing” and “to be in a state of haste” or that it means “staring constantly (towards something)”.
Derivative: عِزِينَ means “in separate groups” i.e., they rush to you from the right and left (i.e., every direction) in separate groups.
Derivative: مَسْبُوقِينَ – To be surpassed or get outdone which means here that nobody will escape our decree or punishment.
Derivative: يَخُوضُوا from حَاضَ means to wade or plunge into something. It is used for immersing oneself and wallowing in useless speech i.e., to run their mouths and say what they want to.
Derivative: يُوفِضُونَ from أَوْفَضَ which means to rush or being made to rush and hasten (towards something).

Derivative: الْأَجْدَاثِ is the plural of جَدَثٌ (Jadath) which is an unmarked grave which also includes all those places where people died but their bodies were never recovered or buried. Its use implies those graves that we are unaware even exist due to the passage of time and that everyone will be raised no matter where or when they died.
Derivative: نُصُبٍ refers to something that has been erected as a finishing line in a competition like a pillar, flag or signpost.
Derivative: تَرْهَقُ means to be piled on top, layer after layer i.e., to be absolutely covered in something.
Derivative: الْمُهْلِ refers to metal like copper, brass and gold that has been melted and turned into liquid.
